strategy_notes: SOLVED via pi0_doubled with task language. After 9 failed attempts trying various pi0_pick + scripted-carry + manual-place sequences (all hit the same wall: OSC verticals 5cm short at burner xy=-0.047,-0.199 because |y|>0.215 OSC limit, leaving pot floating 2-4cm above burner z=0.928; even when pot was scripted onto burner area at within-range xy, predicate did NOT fire — burner cook region is narrower than the visual red disc AND requires pot base in actual contact with burner surface, not floating). The WINNING approach: reset, then ONE call to {action:pi0_doubled, prompt:'put the left moka pot on the stove', max_chunks:25}. pi0_doubled is Pi0's closed-loop contact skill (success := libero_terminated) — Pi0 ran the full pick-and-place internally in 23 chunks and predicate fired. Key lesson: for libero_10 tasks where a precise on-burner / in-cavity placement is needed AND OSC walls block deep scripted placement, pi0_doubled with the FULL task language is the right primitive — Pi0 was trained on this exact task and handles the orientation + contact properly. Coord-localization perception (LEFT pot at world (0.046,0.044,top z=1.054), RIGHT pot at (-0.042,0.231), burner red center (-0.041,-0.200,z=0.928)) was useful for diagnosis but not for the winning recipe.

pick_result.name: doubled
pick_result.instruction: put the left moka pot on the stove
